name: sqlline
title: sqlline
summary: JDBC command-line utility for issuing SQL
description:
JDBC command-line utility for issuing SQL
SQLLine is a pure-Java console based utility for connecting to
relational databases and executing SQL commands.
.
It is similar to other command-line database access utilities like
sqlplus for Oracle, mysql for MySQL, and isql for Sybase/SQL Server.
.
Since it is pure-Java, it is platform independent, and will run on
any platform that can run Java 1.3 or higher.

commands.name: sqlline
commands.description: JDBC command-line utility for issuing SQL

arguments.description: the JDBC URL to connect to
arguments.raw: -u  the JDBC URL to connect to

use_cases: When you need Kali Linux, use sqlline because JDBC command-line utility for issuing SQL.
When the engagement is in the install phase, use sqlline as a cataloged Kali Linux tool with operator ACCEPT/CANCEL gating.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `sqlline [ option... ]` as documented upstream for sqlline.
When chaining Kali Linux tools, pair sqlline with sibling catalog entries sharing its attack_surface/phase_hint.
When validating lab setup, run `sqlline --help` (or the README entrypoint) and confirm required hardware/deps from the catalog.
